rationale: Educ. Code §30.021 governs the purpose and operations of the Texas School for the Blind and Visually Impaired. It contains no provisions related to property tax exemptions or homestead appraisal values, so the proposed Tax Code §11.13(b) change creates no conflict.
quote: The Texas School for the Blind and Visually Impaired is a state agency established to serve as a special school in the continuum of statewide alternative placements for students who are 21 years of age or younger on September 1 of any school year and who have a visual impairment and who may have one or more other disabilities.
_note: No conflict. The rule text concerns special education services for visually impaired students and is unrelated to homestead exemption amounts.
